I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.", "date": "August 01, 2016 at 03:44 AM"}, {"review_id": "reviewid_1067", "user_id": "userid_213", "business_ref": "businessref_89", "rating": "5", "useful": "2", "funny": "0", "cool": "0", "text": "Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too", "date": "June 14, 2021 at 11:39 AM"}, {"review_id": "reviewid_871", "user_id": "userid_616", "business_ref": "businessref_82", "rating": "4", "useful": "0", "funny": "0", "cool": "0", "text": "My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. var_functions.query_db:8.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

args.code:
import json
import pandas as pd

# Read businesses accepting credit cards from file
businesses_file = "file_storage/functions.query_db:14.json"
with open(businesses_file, 'r') as f:
    businesses = json.load(f)

# Parse businesses to extract categories and identify which accept credit cards
business_categories = {}
business_names = {}
credit_accepting_businesses = set()

for biz in businesses:
    bid = biz['business_id']  # format: businessid_XXX
    bref = bid.replace('businessid_', 'businessref_')
    
    business_names[bid] = biz.get('name', 'Unknown')
    
    # Check if accepts credit cards
    attributes = biz.get('attributes', {})
    accepts_credit = False
    if attributes and isinstance(attributes, dict):
        credit_attr = attributes.get('BusinessAcceptsCreditCards')
        if str(credit_attr).lower() == 'true':
            accepts_credit = True
            credit_accepting_businesses.add(bid)
    
    # Extract categories from description
    categories = []
    description = biz.get('description', '')
    
    # Parse description for categories (various patterns)
    desc_lower = description.lower()
    
    if 'providing a range of services in ' in description:
        cat_text = description.split('providing a range of services in ')[1]
        categories_str = cat_text.split('.')[0]
        categories = [cat.strip() for cat in categories_str.split(',')]
    elif 'offers a wide range of services, including ' in description:
        cat_text = description.split('offers a wide range of services, including ')[1]
        categories_str = cat_text.split('.')[0]
        categories = [cat.strip() for cat in categories_str.split(',')]
    elif 'offers a range of services in ' in description:
        cat_text = description.split('offers a range of services in ')[1]
        categories_str = cat_text.split('.')[0]
        categories = [cat.strip() for cat in categories_str.split(',')]
    elif 'offers a range of services including ' in description:
        cat_text = description.split('offers a range of services including ')[1]
        categories_str = cat_text.split('.')[0]
        categories = [cat.strip() for cat in categories_str.split(',')]
    elif 'offers a diverse range of services and products in the fields of ' in description:
        cat_text = description.split('offers a diverse range of services and products in the fields of ')[1]
        categories_str = cat_text.split('.')[0]
        categories = [cat.strip() for cat in categories_str.split(',')]
    elif "offers a delightful array of options ranging from " in description:
        cat_text = description.split("offers a delightful array of options ranging from ")[1]
        categories_str = cat_text.split(', making it')[0] if ', making it' in cat_text else cat_text.split('.')[0]
        categories = [cat.strip() for cat in categories_str.split(',')]
    elif 'category of ' in description:
        cat_text = description.split("category of '")[1] if "category of '" in description else description.split("category of ")[1]
        categories_str = cat_text.split("'")[0] if "'" in cat_text else cat_text.split('.')[0]
        categories = [cat.strip() for cat in categories_str.split(',')]
    
    # Clean and standardize categories
    cleaned_categories = []
    for cat in categories:
        cat = cat.strip().strip("'").strip('"')
        if cat and len(cat) > 0:
            cleaned_categories.append(cat)
    
    business_categories[bid] = cleaned_categories

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps({
    'total_businesses': len(businesses),
    'credit_accepting_count': len(credit_accepting_businesses),
    'sample_businesses': list(credit_accepting_businesses)[:5]
}))

var_functions.query_db:8.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

val_args.code:
import json
import pandas as pd

# Read businesses accepting credit cards from file
businesses_file = "file_storage/functions.query_db:14.json"
with open(businesses_file, 'r') as f:
    businesses = json.load(f)

# Parse businesses to extract categories and identify which accept credit cards
business_categories = {}
business_names = {}
credit_accepting_businesses = set()

for biz in businesses:
    bid = biz['business_id']  # format: businessid_XXX
    bref = bid.replace('businessid_', 'businessref_')
    
    business_names[bid] = biz.get('name', 'Unknown')
    
    # Check if accepts credit cards
    attributes = biz.get('attributes', {})
    accepts_credit = False
    if attributes and isinstance(attributes, dict):
        credit_attr = attributes.get('BusinessAcceptsCreditCards')
        if str(credit_attr).lower() == 'true':
            accepts_credit = True
            credit_accepting_businesses.add(bid)
    
    # Extract categories from description
    categories = []
    description = biz.get('description', '')
    
    # Parse description for categories (various patterns)
    desc_lower = description.lower()
    
    if 'providing a range of services in ' in description:
        cat_text = description.split('providing a range of services in ')[1]
        categories_str = cat_text.split('.')[0]
        categories = [cat.strip() for cat in categories_str.split(',')]
    elif 'offers a wide range of services, including ' in description:
        cat_text = description.split('offers a wide range of services, including ')[1]
        categories_str = cat_text.split('.')[0]
        categories = [cat.strip() for cat in categories_str.split(',')]
    elif 'offers a range of services in ' in description:
        cat_text = description.split('offers a range of services in ')[1]
        categories_str = cat_text.split('.')[0]
        categories = [cat.strip() for cat in categories_str.split(',')]
    elif 'offers a range of services including ' in description:
        cat_text = description.split('offers a range of services including ')[1]
        categories_str = cat_text.split('.')[0]
        categories = [cat.strip() for cat in categories_str.split(',')]
    elif 'offers a diverse range of services and products in the fields of ' in description:
        cat_text = description.split('offers a diverse range of services and products in the fields of ')[1]
        categories_str = cat_text.split('.')[0]
        categories = [cat.strip() for cat in categories_str.split(',')]
    elif "offers a delightful array of options ranging from " in description:
        cat_text = description.split("offers a delightful array of options ranging from ")[1]
        categories_str = cat_text.split(', making it')[0] if ', making it' in cat_text else cat_text.split('.')[0]
        categories = [cat.strip() for cat in categories_str.split(',')]
    elif 'category of ' in description:
        cat_text = description.split("category of '")[1] if "category of '" in description else description.split("category of ")[1]
        categories_str = cat_text.split("'")[0] if "'" in cat_text else cat_text.split('.')[0]
        categories = [cat.strip() for cat in categories_str.split(',')]
    
    # Clean and standardize categories
    cleaned_categories = []
    for cat in categories:
        cat = cat.strip().strip("'").strip('"')
        if cat and len(cat) > 0:
            cleaned_categories.append(cat)
    
    business_categories[bid] = cleaned_categories

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps({
    'total_businesses': len(businesses),
    'credit_accepting_count': len(credit_accepting_businesses),
    'sample_businesses': list(credit_accepting_businesses)[:5]
}))

var_functions.query_db:8.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

args.code:
import json
import os

# Let's check what files are available
files = []
for root, dirs, filenames in os.walk('.'):
    for filename in filenames:
        files.append(os.path.join(root, filename))

# Find JSON files that might contain our business data
json_files = [f for f in files if f.endswith('.json')]
print(f"JSON files found: {json_files}")

# Let's also check the locals for any lists
list_vars = []
for key, value in locals().items():
    if isinstance(value, list) and len(value) > 0:
        list_vars.append((key, len(value)))

print(f"Lists in local variables: {list_vars}")
print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps({
    'json_files': json_files,
    'list_vars': list_vars
}))

args.code:
import json

# Check the business data we have
businesses = var_functions.query_db_18
print(f"Number of businesses: {len(businesses)}")

# Count credit card accepting businesses
credit_accepting = []
for biz in businesses:
    attributes = biz.get('attributes', {})
    if attributes and isinstance(attributes, dict):
        if str(attributes.get('BusinessAcceptsCreditCards')).lower() == 'true':
            credit_accepting.append(biz)

print(f"Credit accepting businesses: {len(credit_accepting)}")
print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps({
    'total': len(businesses),
    'credit_accepting': len(credit_accepting),
    'sample': credit_accepting[:2]
}))

val_args.code:
import json

# Check the business data we have
businesses = var_functions.query_db_18
print(f"Number of businesses: {len(businesses)}")

# Count credit card accepting businesses
credit_accepting = []
for biz in businesses:
    attributes = biz.get('attributes', {})
    if attributes and isinstance(attributes, dict):
        if str(attributes.get('BusinessAcceptsCreditCards')).lower() == 'true':
            credit_accepting.append(biz)

print(f"Credit accepting businesses: {len(credit_accepting)}")
print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps({
    'total': len(businesses),
    'credit_accepting': len(credit_accepting),
    'sample': credit_accepting[:2]
}))

var_functions.query_db:8.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.

var_functions.query_db:8.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

args.code:
import json

# Get the businesses from the last query
businesses = locals()['var_functions.query_db:30']

# Parse categories for credit-accepting businesses
category_counts = {}

for biz in businesses:
    description = biz.get('description', '')
    categories = []
    
    # Pattern 1: "providing a range of services in"
    if 'providing a range of services in ' in description:
        cat_part = description.split('providing a range of services in ')[1].split('.')[0]
        categories = [c.strip() for c in cat_part.split(',')]
    # Pattern 2: "offers a wide range of services, including"
    elif 'offers a wide range of services, including ' in description:
        cat_part = description.split('offers a wide range of services, including ')[1].split('.')[0]
        categories = [c.strip() for c in cat_part.split(',')]
    # Pattern 3: "offers a range of services including"
    elif 'offers a range of services including ' in description:
        cat_part = description.split('offers a range of services including ')[1].split('.')[0]
        categories = [c.strip() for c in cat_part.split(',')]
    # Pattern 4: "offers a range of services in"
    elif 'offers a range of services in ' in description:
        cat_part = description.split('offers a range of services in ')[1].split('.')[0]
        categories = [c.strip() for c in cat_part.split(',')]
    # Pattern 5: "offers a diverse range of services and products in the fields of"
    elif 'offers a diverse range of services and products in the fields of ' in description:
        cat_part = description.split('offers a diverse range of services and products in the fields of ')[1].split('.')[0]
        categories = [c.strip() for c in cat_part.split(',')]
    # Pattern 6: "offers a delightful array of options ranging from"
    elif 'offers a delightful array of options ranging from ' in description:
        cat_part = description.split('offers a delightful array of options ranging from ')[1].split(', making it')[0]
        categories = [c.strip() for c in cat_part.split(',')]
    # Pattern 7: "category of"
    elif 'category of ' in description:
        if 'category of \'' in description:
            cat_part = description.split('category of \'')[1].split('\'')[0]
        else:
            cat_part = description.split('category of ')[1].split('.')[0]
        categories = [c.strip() for c in cat_part.split(',')]
    
    # Count valid categories
    for cat in categories:
        cat_clean = cat.strip().strip("'\" ")
        if cat_clean:
            category_counts[cat_clean] = category_counts.get(cat_clean, 0) + 1

# Sort categories by count descending
top_categories = sorted(category_counts.items(), key=lambda x: x[1], reverse=True)

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps({
    'credit_accepting_count': len(businesses),
    'top_categories': top_categories
}))

var_functions.query_db:8.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

        cat_clean = cat.strip("'\" ")
        if cat_clean:
            category_counts[cat_clean] = category_counts.get(cat_clean, 0) + 1

# Find the category with most businesses
top_category = max(category_counts, key=category_counts.get) if category_counts else None
top_count = category_counts.get(top_category, 0) if top_category else 0

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps({
    'top_category': top_category,
    'business_count': top_count,
    'category_counts': category_counts
}))

var_functions.query_db:8.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). val_args.code:
import json

# Get the businesses from the last query that accept credit cards
businesses = locals()['var_functions.query_db:30']

# Parse categories for credit-accepting businesses
category_counts = {}

for biz in businesses:
    description = biz.get('description', '')
    categories = []
    
    # Pattern: "providing a range of services in "
    if 'providing a range of services in ' in description:
        parts = description.split('providing a range of services in ')
        cat_part = parts[1].split('.')[0]
        categories = [c.strip() for c in cat_part.split(',')]
    # Pattern: "offers a wide range of services, including "
    elif 'offers a wide range of services, including ' in description:
        parts = description.split('offers a wide range of services, including ')
        cat_part = parts[1].split('.')[0]
        categories = [c.strip() for c in cat_part.split(',')]
    # Pattern: "offers a range of services including "
    elif 'offers a range of services including ' in description:
        parts = description.split('offers a range of services including ')
        cat_part = parts[1].split('.')[0]
        categories = [c.strip() for c in cat_part.split(',')]
    # Pattern: "offers a range of services in "
    elif 'offers a range of services in ' in description:
        parts = description.split('offers a range of services in ')
        cat_part = parts[1].split('.')[0]
        categories = [c.strip() for c in cat_part.split(',')]
    # Pattern: "offers a diverse range... in the fields of "
    elif 'in the fields of ' in description:
        parts = description.split('in the fields of ')
        cat_part = parts[1].split('.')[0]
        categories = [c.strip() for c in cat_part.split(',')]
    # Pattern: "category of "
    elif 'category of ' in description:
        if "category of '" in description:
            parts = description.split("category of '")
            cat_part = parts[1].split("'")[0]
        else:
            parts = description.split('category of ')
            cat_part = parts[1].split('.')[0]
        categories = [c.strip() for c in cat_part.split(',')]
    
    # Count valid categories
    for cat in categories:
        cat_clean = cat.strip("'\" ")
        if cat_clean:
            category_counts[cat_clean] = category_counts.get(cat_clean, 0) + 1

# Find the category with most businesses
top_category = max(category_counts, key=category_counts.get) if category_counts else None
top_count = category_counts.get(top_category, 0) if top_category else 0

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps({
    'top_category': top_category,
    'business_count': top_count,
    'category_counts': category_counts
}))

var_functions.query_db:8.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

var_functions.query_db:8.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

This location is not one of my favorites people here get pretty rude sometimes no one looks happy it's hit or miss with the food sometimes it's awesome other times terrible I always get honey walnut shrimp for an extra 1.25 which is retarded and Beijing beef I enjoy the rice n the noodles but idk do if u dare
Terrible service. I was charged twice for online order and they refused to refund me. Numerous times this has happened

var_functions.query_db:8.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

This location is not one of my favorites people here get pretty rude sometimes no one looks happy it's hit or miss with the food sometimes it's awesome other times terrible I always get honey walnut shrimp for an extra 1.25 which is retarded and Beijing beef I enjoy the rice n the noodles but idk do if u dare
Terrible service. I was charged twice for online order and they refused to refund me. Numerous times this has happened

I've purchased about 10 used cars in the last 25 years. This has been the worst experience by far. Test drove and purchased a used car on 3/31 for my 16 year old daughter, the salesman knew it was for her. Bought the 3 year 'bumper to bumper' warranty the finance guy said was the same as if it \"just came off the showroom floor\". They needed to remove pin stripes and fix a tire pressure sensor so they agreed to deliver it; we live almost two hours away. Met them after dark near the highway on 4/2; the check engine light came on on our way home. Texted a pic to the salesman and called him the next day -- no response. Left several voice mails for the warranty/finance guy - no response. Took it to a friend at local dealer who hooked it up to the diagnostic machine and said it was the after-market high-flow air intake that caused the warning light. I would not have purchased the car had I known that. Then another warning light - reduced engine performance, reduce clutch use. To make a very long story short - clutch goes, daughter gets stranded on busy highway, vehicle towed back to Ford of Port Richey; lots of promises about fixing it and being at the front of the service line, zero follow up, 53 days of ownership, 31 days out of commission/in shop and the story ends with a bill for $2000, even though they ultimately acknowledged one of their managers drove the car for three days because the check engine light kept coming on before they sold it. I won't even mention the cracked windshield we saw the morning after they delivered - just turn it in on your insurance. I understand it is free but still counts as a claim.   I'm just glad 16 year old girls are patient and understanding.", "date": "08 Jun 2019, 07:10"}, {"review_id": "reviewid_1928", "user_id": "userid_1151", "business_ref": "businessref_9", "rating": "5", "useful": "0", "funny": "0", "cool": "0", "text": "I had a great experience at Coffee House Too. I highly recommend this restaurant and will definitely be coming back!", "date": "2016-07-18 21:37:00"}, {"review_id": "reviewid_1790", "user_id": "userid_668", "business_ref": "businessref_26", "rating": "1", "useful": "0", "funny": "0", "cool": "0", "text": "Breakfast is very expensive at $7 for a breakfast sandwich meal with oj. My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

var_functions.query_db:8.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

args.code:
import json
import pandas as pd

# Load data
businesses = locals()['var_functions.query_db:30']
reviews_file = 'file_storage/functions.query_db:48.json'

with open(reviews_file, 'r') as f:
    reviews = json.load(f)

# Map business_id to categories for credit-accepting businesses
credit_business_cats = {}
credit_business_ids = set()

for biz in businesses:
    bid = biz['business_id']
    credit_business_ids.add(bid)
    bref = bid.replace('businessid_', 'businessref_')
    
    desc = biz.get('description', '')
    
    # Extract categories - simplified parsing
    categories = []
    if 'in ' in desc:
        parts = desc.split('in ')
        if len(parts) > 1:
            temp = parts[1].split('.')[0]
            categories = [c.strip() for c in temp.split(',')]
    
    clean_cats = []
    for cat in categories:
        clean = cat.strip("'\" ")
        if clean and len(clean) < 50:  # avoid long strings
            clean_cats.append(clean)
    
    credit_business_cats[bref] = clean_cats

# Count categories
from collections import defaultdict
cat_counts = defaultdict(int)
biz_per_category = defaultdict(list)

for bref, cats in credit_business_cats.items():
    for cat in cats:
        cat_counts[cat] += 1
        biz_per_category[cat].append(bref)

# Find top category
top_cat = max(cat_counts.items(), key=lambda x: x[1]) if cat_counts else (None, 0)

# Get reviews for businesses in top category
if top_cat[0]:
    target_bref = biz_per_category[top_cat[0]]
    
    ratings = []
    for review in reviews:
        if review['business_ref'] in target_bref:
            try:
                ratings.append(int(review['rating']))
            except:
                pass
    
    avg_rating = sum(ratings) / len(ratings) if ratings else 0
    
    result = {
        'top_category': top_cat[0],
        'business_count': top_cat[1],
        'avg_rating': round(avg_rating, 2),
        'total_ratings': len(ratings)
    }
else:
    result = {'error': 'No categories found'}

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps(result))

val_args.code:
import json
import pandas as pd

# Load data
businesses = locals()['var_functions.query_db:30']
reviews_file = 'file_storage/functions.query_db:48.json'

with open(reviews_file, 'r') as f:
    reviews = json.load(f)

# Map business_id to categories for credit-accepting businesses
credit_business_cats = {}
credit_business_ids = set()

for biz in businesses:
    bid = biz['business_id']
    credit_business_ids.add(bid)
    bref = bid.replace('businessid_', 'businessref_')
    
    desc = biz.get('description', '')
    
    # Extract categories - simplified parsing
    categories = []
    if 'in ' in desc:
        parts = desc.split('in ')
        if len(parts) > 1:
            temp = parts[1].split('.')[0]
            categories = [c.strip() for c in temp.split(',')]
    
    clean_cats = []
    for cat in categories:
        clean = cat.strip("'\" ")
        if clean and len(clean) < 50:  # avoid long strings
            clean_cats.append(clean)
    
    credit_business_cats[bref] = clean_cats

# Count categories
from collections import defaultdict
cat_counts = defaultdict(int)
biz_per_category = defaultdict(list)

for bref, cats in credit_business_cats.items():
    for cat in cats:
        cat_counts[cat] += 1
        biz_per_category[cat].append(bref)

# Find top category
top_cat = max(cat_counts.items(), key=lambda x: x[1]) if cat_counts else (None, 0)

# Get reviews for businesses in top category
if top_cat[0]:
    target_bref = biz_per_category[top_cat[0]]
    
    ratings = []
    for review in reviews:
        if review['business_ref'] in target_bref:
            try:
                ratings.append(int(review['rating']))
            except:
                pass
    
    avg_rating = sum(ratings) / len(ratings) if ratings else 0
    
    result = {
        'top_category': top_cat[0],
        'business_count': top_cat[1],
        'avg_rating': round(avg_rating, 2),
        'total_ratings': len(ratings)
    }
else:
    result = {'error': 'No categories found'}

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps(result))

var_functions.query_db:8.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

var_functions.query_db:8.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

This location is not one of my favorites people here get pretty rude sometimes no one looks happy it's hit or miss with the food sometimes it's awesome other times terrible I always get honey walnut shrimp for an extra 1.25 which is retarded and Beijing beef I enjoy the rice n the noodles but idk do if u dare
Terrible service. I was charged twice for online order and they refused to refund me. Numerous times this has happened
